inputNotes.styleTarget: config.json's priming.styleTarget was unusable literal text, so the pass used story-arc.md, .afternoon/style-guide.json, the POV voice sheets, and chapter24/final.md as the operative style benchmark
inputNotes.missingReference: stories/the-plague-road/prose-sample.md is referenced by the arc/style materials but is missing at that path

examples: Scene I opening now picks up chapter 24's three-tap ending instead of restarting cold at the blade pulse.
Jaina's confession line changed from the abstract 'the part that mattered' to 'what being right could do' so the admission sounds like her, not an essay heading.
Scene I wall-report now uses 'leaves a trace' / 'devotion stays' rather than softer, more generalized phrasing.
observations: Read Scene I against Jaina's voice sheet: precise, educated, controlled, but not clinical. Most of the section already fit.
Checked paragraph breathing against the style guide's dialogue-forward cadence. One-line beats remain for impact; no flattening edit needed there.
Flagged the opening as slightly detached from the prior chapter's last image. Fixed with the three-tap carryover.
Flagged Jaina's 'the part that mattered' as too thesis-like for prison-wall confession. Replaced with a sentence closer to spoken self-indictment.
Checked Sylvanas scene for over-theatrical diction. Only one light trim was needed: 'earned a glimpse' reads cleaner than 'earned a look' in that demonic speech beat.

examples: Scene II now reads 'So Jaina felt it too' instead of the colder category label 'the mage,' preserving Sylvanas's actual current relation to her.
Sylvanas's listening beat now anchors danger to tone she can hear, not a more summary-like narrator statement.
observations: Tested the opening blade-read sentence by sentence: all knowledge remains in Jaina's sensory contact with stone and spellwork.
Checked whether the guard passage claimed impossible certainty. It stays within what Jaina can infer from Frostmourne's brush and the guard's gait.
Reviewed the Mal'Ganis entrance for taxonomy-before-perception. The prose still earns the reveal through body, weight, voice, and only later pattern recognition.
Flagged 'the mage' in Sylvanas's internal narration as a slight distancing reset rather than true current POV language. Fixed to 'Jaina.'
Flagged the 'Tone was enough...' line as close to author-summary. Recast it into Sylvanas's immediate practical read.

examples: Cross-chapter bridge now explicitly carries over the three light taps from chapter 24's close.
Sylvanas's internal naming keeps the chapter 24 partnership state intact instead of sliding back to a colder, earlier-distance label.
observations: Checked chapter 24 ending against chapter 25 opening. The prison night, adjacent cells, and blade countdown remain continuous.
Verified Frostmourne stays an active background threat from line one, matching the plan and active-frostmourne memory thread.
Checked Jaina/Sylvanas relationship state against the relationship memory file. The wall-code intimacy and hard candor are consistent.
Reviewed the five-face reveal against the new-grain-seller memory thread. It lands as recognition and compression, not first-time exposition.
Verified no prison-release drift, no timeline skip, and no contradiction of Lordaeron palace geography.

examples: Scene I opening now begins with a longer cross-chapter sentence before dropping into the short count beat.
The wall-interrogation cluster around lines 141-171 was reweighted to break an accumulating chain of ultra-short exchanges.
'It hears accuracy' became 'It hears what's true' for stronger cadence and plainer spoken force.
observations: Reviewed the sentence-band scan findings from the short-run cluster in Jaina's confession and tactical report.
The chapter still intentionally uses hard short beats around the Frostmourne count; those stayed because they carry threat.
Flagged the 'How far / Past / If / Depends / Linear / No' cluster as starting to drum too evenly. Broke the pattern with a longer answer turn.
Flagged the truth-trigger exchange as too stepwise right before the pulse hit. Combined the final prompt-and-answer into one interrupted line.
Checked Scene II for opener monotony after the visitor arrives. Existing variation was strong enough; no broad rewrite needed.

examples: Scene I now opens on the prior chapter's three-tap residue, tightening chapter-to-chapter continuity.
The truth-trigger exchange now lands in clearer causal order: question, partial answer, early pulse.
Jaina remains an audible witness in Scene II through breathing/tone continuity instead of vanishing behind the wall.
observations: Read the chapter against the plan's chapterBridge: same prison night, same cells, same countdown. Delivered.
Checked Scene I transitions for causal motion rather than temporal glue. No 'later/then' scaffolding survived.
Flagged the truth-test exchange because the cause-and-effect line was slightly muffled by segmented dialogue. Clarified it.
Checked Scene II opening against the end of Scene I: Frostmourne pressure and shared imprisonment still carry through the seam.
Verified the ending handoff to wall-code preserves the next-action logic: phrase first, five taps second, rest withheld for safer transmission.

examples: Trimmed 'leaves something behind' to the firmer 'leaves a trace.'
Trimmed the slightly generalized 'the part that mattered' into more concrete self-accusation.
observations: Checked for scene-opening throat-clearing. The chapter's existing openings were already sharp; the work was mostly refinement, not replacement.
Checked for biography re-insertions around Frostmourne, Arthas, and the plague network. No major recap bloat survived.
Flagged two vague phrases that softened pressure in Jaina's wall exchange and confession. Both were tightened.
Checked for parallel-structure fatigue in the Mal'Ganis recognition cascade. The repetition there is purposeful mnemonic hammering, so it stayed.
Checked for emotional telling without bodily correlate. Existing body cues were already strong enough in both POV sections.

examples: 'It hears accuracy' -> 'It hears what's true' to keep Jaina's prison-wall speech plain and spoken.
observations: Scanned all quoted dialogue for bureaucratic or clinical contamination from the hitlist. None of the major banned phrases appeared.
Jaina's dialogue stays educated but field-usable; she sounds like a mage under pressure, not a report.
Sylvanas's dialogue stays clipped and tactical without collapsing into monosyllabic caricature.
Mal'Ganis's dialogue remains formal because the speaker is inhuman and courtly, but it does not slide into modern institutional jargon.
Only one spoken line needed register adjustment: 'accuracy' sounded too diagnostic in the mouth and was simplified.
